reasoning: I need to determine the relationship between d1, d2, d3, and total_distance. Currently, d1=10, d2=20, d3=30, and total_distance=60. I will increase d1 by 1 to see how total_distance changes.

From the initial state (d1=10, d2=20, d3=30, total=60) and the state after increasing d1 by 10 (d1=20, d2=20, d3=30, total=70), I observed that increasing d1 by 10 resulted in an increase of 10 in total_distance. I have observed that increasing d1 by 10 increased total_distance by 10, and increasing d2 by 10 increased total_distance by 10. This strongly suggests that total_distance is the sum of d1, d2, and d3. To be thorough, I will test changing d3 as well to confirm the relationship holds for the third variable.

I have tested increasing d1, d2, and d3 individually. This linear relationship for all three variables strongly indicates that total_distance is the sum of d1, d2, and d3. The current state (d1=20, d2=30, d3=40, total=90) confirms 20+30+40=90.
